concerns.text: FR-008 requires validating the 'Generalization Gap' against an independent ground-truth metric (e.g., human evaluations). The plan.md, research.md, and data-model.md contain no task, data entity, or method to acquire or process human evaluation data. The plan relies solely on VLM reward models, leaving FR-008 unbacked.

SC-002 requires measuring statistical power against a minimum sample size for a medium effect size. The plan.md (Section 4) explicitly states the 500-prompt target is infeasible (50 hours vs 6-hour limit) and proposes reducing the sample size or reporting a limitation. No plan element defines how the 'minimum sample size' will be determined or measured to satisfy the power requirement before execution.

SC-005 requires flagging prompts where score variance exceeds a 'predefined threshold'. The plan.md (Section 3.3) mentions flagging prompts with variance > threshold but does not define the threshold value, the calculation method (e.g., per-prompt across 3 images), or the specific action taken (e.g., exclusion, manual review queue). The data-model.md lacks a field to store this variance flag or threshold status.
